title: bassia scoparia var. trichophylla
intro: Bassia scoparia var. trichophylla, also known as Japanese knotweed or Mexican poppy, is a perennial plant native to East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The health benefits of Bassia scoparia var. trichophylla include reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting digestion. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
